codex:
VERDICT: FIXABLE

DIRECTIVE: In core.json, replace the support-switch claim in lem:parametric-gain-support-cells with the following exact criterion and make its proof use identical language: “Fix the stated lexicographic tie-breaking rule and let \(\sigma_E(C)\) denote the selected, lexicographically first maximizing support on an open comparison-sign cell \(C\). Let \(\gamma_0\) be an interior boundary with adjacent cells \(C_-\) and \(C_+\). A named ordered pair \(\mathbf H\to\mathbf K\), with \(\mathbf H\ne\mathbf K\), is a genuine selected-support switch at \(\gamma_0\) if and only if \(F_{E,\mathbf H}(\gamma_0)=F_{E,\mathbf K}(\gamma_0)=\max_{\mathbf L}F_{E,\mathbf L}(\gamma_0)\), \(\sigma_E(C_-)=\mathbf H\), and \(\sigma_E(C_+)=\mathbf K\). Hence the pairwise cross-product polynomial vanishes at \(\gamma_0\), but vanishing and dominance alone are not sufficient; the two named supports must be the respective adjacent-cell selections. Reversing the orientation exchanges \(\mathbf H\) and \(\mathbf K\).” Replace the repeated iff in thm:operational-joint-certificate-and-gain-trichotomy with this same named-pair adjacent-selection criterion and synchronize its proof rather than merely referring to the old lemma wording. Remove ass:prespecified-score from that theorem’s depends_on list and from the assumption’s corresponding used_by entry; do not add a validity conclusion to the computational theorem and do not delete the assumption, which remains available to lem:finite-joint-validity and inferential-validity prose. Apply the same redundant-dependency cleanup wholesale to lem:negative-gain-cell-witness: remove ass:prespecified-score from depends_on and delete the proof’s claim that prespecification is needed for the numerical input’s admissibility. Regenerate writeup.tex from the corrected source so the lemma statement and proof, theorem statement and proof, dependency metadata, and negative-gain proof all agree. Make no other substantive changes: preserve the Fisher-sharp-null Rosenbaum product-law scope, rational encoded-input domain, field headline, strict-gain/equality/negative-gain trichotomy, and no-full-product-enumeration certificate, without strengthening assumptions or changing definitions.

WHY: A root tie and joint dominance only show that both named supports belong to the argmax set at the boundary; a third support may be selected on either adjacent cell. Fixed lexicographic tie-breaking plus requiring \(\mathbf H\) and \(\mathbf K\) themselves to be the respective adjacent selections gives the exact necessary-and-sufficient named-pair switch criterion. Conditional prespecification affects randomization-inference validity, not optimization of already supplied rational scores, so it is redundant in both the computational theorem and the purely numerical negative-gain witness.
why: FIXABLE convergence: exact adjacent-cell named-support selection repairs the overstated iff without weakening the field headline, and prespecified-score remains solely where inferential validity consumes it. Inject this verbatim direction via d0_directive.ts and rerun D0→D0.5.

codex:
VERDICT: FIXABLE_FIELD

CLOSEST_COMPARATOR: Rosenbaum (1988), extending Rosenbaum (1987), gives finite-sample sensitivity bounds for matched sets with arbitrary outcomes only for sign-score statistics; Rosenbaum–Krieger (1990), as explicitly restated in Pimentel–Huang (2024, Theorem 3), reduces an exact one-sided worst-case calculation to score-monotone binary hidden-bias vectors U_+. This does not cover the nonmonotone high sets required by an opposite-tail union. Rosenbaum 1988 DOI 10.1093/biomet/75.3.577; Rosenbaum–Krieger 1990 DOI 10.1080/01621459.1990.10476226; Pimentel–Huang 2024 DOI 10.1093/jrsssb/qkae033. The closest shared-confounder optimizer is Fogarty–Small (2016): its QCQP enforces one assignment-probability vector across multiple outcomes and uniformly improves separate analyses, but optimizes asymptotic mean/variance standardized deviates, not the finite randomization probability P{|S|≥t}, and supplies neither an exact opposite-tail p-value nor an exact rational certificate. DOI 10.1080/01621459.2015.1120675.

DIRECTIVE: Reframe the field-level claim away from generic MILP certification: add a proved theorem thm:opposite-tail-capability-separation-and-hardness under the existing frozen assumptions stating (i) the exact Rosenbaum/Rosenbaum–Krieger one-sided score-monotone candidate reduction does not extend to \(A_J\), witnessed by the existing \(n=1,m=4,\Gamma=2,t=1,s=(-2,0,1,2)\) instance, where the unique maximizing high set is the nonmonotone event set \(\{-2,1,2\}\), giving \(p_J=6/7\), while every upper- or lower-score-monotone high set gives at most \(5/6\); (ii) the Fogarty–Small moment-QCQP cannot determine \(p_J\), proved at \(n=1,m=4,\Gamma=1,t=1\) by the rational score vectors \((-1,-1,1,1)\) and \((-7/5,-1/5,1/5,7/5)\), which have the same mean \(0\) and variance \(1\) but joint-tail probabilities \(1\) and \(1/2\); and (iii) exact computation of \(p_J\) is \(\#P\)-hard even for reversal-symmetric matched pairs at \(\Gamma=1\) with integer scores, by reducing \(\#\mathrm{PARTITION}\): set pair \(i\)’s scores to \((-a_i,a_i)\) and \(t=1\), so \(p_J=1-N_0/2^n\), where \(N_0\) is the number of zero signed sums. State only \(\#P\)-hardness of exact value computation and polynomial verification in transcript length—do not claim an unconditional exponential certificate lower bound. Split these claims into supporting lemmas if needed, add their exact dependencies and citations to the DAG, and make thm:operational-joint-certificate-and-gain-trichotomy depend on them while retaining its current exact MILP proof as the constructive upper bound. Rewrite the TL;DR, all project_justification fields, related_work, comparator table, novelty justification, technical limitation, and corresponding writeup.tex prose so the headline is the exact capability separation plus matching hardness/verified exponential-time construction, explicitly naming Rosenbaum (1988), Rosenbaum–Krieger (1990)/Pimentel–Huang (2024, Theorem 3), and Fogarty–Small (2016); delete the unsupported blanket ‘no published result’ claim and do not present finite vertex reduction, non-materialization of a product table, or ordinary branch-and-bound as the novelty. Also carry forward and actually apply the prior mandatory directive: replace the overstated support-switch iff in lem:parametric-gain-support-cells and thm:operational-joint-certificate-and-gain-trichotomy by the exact fixed-lexicographic named-pair adjacent-cell selection criterion, remove ass:prespecified-score from the computational theorem and lem:negative-gain-cell-witness dependencies while retaining it for lem:finite-joint-validity, and synchronize proofs and prose.

WHY: The present MILP theorem alone is below the field floor, but the same machinery already contains a rigorous separation: opposite-tail calibration can require a hidden-bias pattern excluded by exact one-sided monotone reductions, and the closest common-law QCQP discards distributional information needed for the exact tail. The elementary #PARTITION reduction also explains why an exact exponential-time, checkable construction is substantively appropriate rather than an efficiency advance. These are theorem-level claims under the same assumptions, with concrete counterexamples and no unsupported priority assertion.
why: FIXABLE_FIELD: generic MILP certification is not the advance; the faithful field headline is an exact capability separation plus #P-hardness and a checkable exponential-time upper construction. Inject the source-level theorem/DAG/prose direction and explicitly re-enter D0.

and define \(G=p_B^{\rm obs}-p_J\) and \(\mathfrak R_{\rm strict}=\{p_J\le\alpha<p_B^{\rm obs}\}\) using \(z_{\rm obs}\) with \(t_{\rm obs}=|S(z_{\rm obs};r)|\). Add a non-novel, credited helper \`lem:finite-sharp-null-two-sided-validity\` proving conditional super-uniformity of both \(p_J(Z)\) and \(p_B^{\rm obs}(Z)\) for every true \(P_0\in\mathcal P_\Gamma\); do not restore it as the frozen root or reintroduce \`ass:prespecified-score\`, because the score table is fixed under the existing sharp-null conditioning. Keep the operational theorem computational, and put inferential claims in a separate reporting corollary depending on this validity lemma. Rewrite the parametric gain/equality/loss cells and verifier inequalities by observed-sign cell: for \(S_{\rm obs}>0\), \(p_B^{\rm obs}=\min(1,2p_+)\); for \(S_{\rm obs}<0\), \(p_B^{\rm obs}=\min(1,2p_-)\); for zero, both calibrations equal one. Preserve the fixed-lexicographic adjacent-support-switch criterion. Extend \`def:gamma-two-witness\` by \(z_{\rm obs}=(5,2)\), so \(S_{\rm obs}=t_{\rm obs}=6>0\), and retain
\[
p_+=4/15,\quad p_-=2/7,\quad p_J=3/8,\quad p_B^{\rm obs}=8/15,\quad G=19/120.
\]
Retain exact \(\Gamma=1\) equality under reversal symmetry. Replace the invalid old loss fixture by \(n=1,m=4,\Gamma=2,s=(-2,-1,0,1),z_{\rm obs}=4,t_{\rm obs}=1\), which gives \(p_+=2/5,p_-=2/3,p_J=6/7,p_B^{\rm obs}=4/5,G=-2/35\), thereby preserving a genuine loss cell. Update symbols, definitions, theorem statements and proofs, reverse dependencies, comparator table, TL;DR, justification, interpretation, scope, and writeup so “gain” means a pointwise smaller valid p-value/decision discordance, never uniform power improvement; any power comparison requires an alternative-law probability calculation not present here. Keep [Zhao (2019)](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/01621459.2018.1429277) only as a primary informed-by anchor and do not attribute the signless rule to conventional practice. Preserve the v5 durable-root swap, comparator positioning, removal of redundant computational score assumptions, real-\(\gamma\) capability theorem versus rational exact-computation scope, and fixed-design implicit-traversal formulation. D0 must regenerate and verify \`core.json\`, \`writeup.tex\`, PDF, all dependency metadata, both validity proofs, the \(\Gamma=1\) equality, the \(19/120\) positive fixture, the revised \(-2/35\) loss fixture, and the unchanged capability/hardness theorem before D0.5.

WHY: For \(\Gamma>1\), the full high set yields the uniform relative-interior point, so it is not an extreme point; keeping it only as a redundant MILP encoding preserves all optimization results. The current signless \(p_B=2\min(p_+,p_-)\) is not a valid hidden-bias p-value: in the existing \((-2,0,1,2)\), \(\Gamma=2\) example, its level-\(4/5\) rejection set has worst-case probability \(p_J=6/7>4/5\). The observed-sign version is valid by Bonferroni: each directional rejection event has probability at most \(\alpha/2\), so their disjoint union has probability at most \(\alpha\). Likewise \(p_J(z)\ge P_0\{|S(Z)|\ge|S(z)|\}\) for every \(P_0\in\mathcal P_\Gamma\), and the ordinary tail calibration is super-uniform. Thus \(p_J\) is a valid shared-law two-sided sensitivity p-value. That fact follows elementarily from sharp-null calibration, but the current canonical core contains no proved validity node; a short credited helper is therefore required if the paper retains explicit validity or reporting claims. Neither validity result implies global power dominance, while the positive, equality, and loss numerical cells above remain exact.

Reset and extend the same-angle D-1.2 revise runway; no pivot and no F.
